rubric.name: Short Film Story Rubric
rubric.intended_use: Evaluate early short-film ideas and structured story plans for 5-20 minute films.

criteria.name: Story Clarity
criteria.description: How clearly the story communicates its premise, central situation, and progression.
criteria.evaluation_guidance: Look for a focused premise, understandable setup, clear beginning-middle-ending movement, and a story question the audience can follow.
common_mistakes: The idea is only a mood or theme without a clear situation.
Too many plot threads compete for attention.
The audience cannot tell what changes from beginning to end.
Important context is explained too late or not at all.

criteria.name: Character
criteria.description: How well the protagonist is defined through motivation, behavior, and choice.
criteria.evaluation_guidance: Assess whether the protagonist has a visible want, an emotional need, a reason to act now, and a meaningful choice that reveals character.
common_mistakes: The protagonist is passive throughout the story.
The character wants something vague or invisible.
Motivation is explained but not dramatized.
The protagonist does not make a meaningful choice.

criteria.name: Conflict
criteria.description: How effectively the story creates pressure, obstacles, and dramatic movement.
criteria.evaluation_guidance: Evaluate whether the conflict is specific, filmable, connected to the protagonist's goal, and strong enough to sustain a short film without becoming overcomplicated.
common_mistakes: The conflict is too abstract to show on screen.
The obstacle is solved too easily.
The story relies on coincidence instead of character decisions.
There is tension, but it does not escalate.

criteria.name: Emotional Impact
criteria.description: How strongly the story creates a specific emotional journey for the audience.
criteria.evaluation_guidance: Look for emotional stakes, a clear emotional turn, specificity in relationships or situations, and moments that allow the audience to feel rather than simply understand.
common_mistakes: The story tells the audience what to feel instead of earning it.
The emotional goal is generic, such as simply being sad or happy.
The relationship dynamics are underdeveloped.
The film has events but no emotional change.

criteria.name: Ending
criteria.description: How well the ending resolves, reframes, or completes the short film's central question.
criteria.evaluation_guidance: Assess whether the ending feels earned, expresses the emotional turn, avoids over-explanation, and leaves the audience with a strong final image or action.
common_mistakes: The ending arrives abruptly without setup.
The ending explains the theme through dialogue instead of dramatizing it.
The final moment does not connect to the protagonist's choice.
The story continues too long after the emotional turn has landed.

criteria.name: Production Feasibility
criteria.description: How practical the story is for a small short-film production.
criteria.evaluation_guidance: Consider cast size, number of locations, required effects, time period, props, crowd scenes, and whether the core idea can be filmed with limited resources.
common_mistakes: The idea requires too many locations for a short production.
The cast is larger than the story needs.
Expensive visual effects carry the concept instead of character and conflict.
The story would work better as a feature because it needs too much setup.
